title: clinopodium serpyllifolium subsp. serpyllifolium
intro: Clinopodium serpyllifolium subsp. serpyllifolium, also known as Rock Catmint or Clinopodium, is a perennial plant species in the mint family Lamiaceae, characterized by its small, white, and fragrant flowers. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. Clinopodium serpyllifolium subsp. serpyllifolium is said to have several health benefits, including reducing stress, relieving digestive issues, and supporting respiratory health. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine in various forms, including salves, creams, and ointments.
